text.title: Stripe Webhook Hardening Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Stripe Webhook Hardening methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Signature verification MUST cap the Stripe-Signature timestamp tolerance at 5 minutes; longer windows enable replay attacks of captured events.
rule.rationale: Default SDK tolerance is 5 minutes; making it bigger to mask clock drift opens a replay window.

rule.statement: Webhook secret MUST be rotated on a documented cadence (<=90 days) or on any suspected exposure; the rotation runbook MUST land in the repo.
rule.rationale: Long-lived secrets are the most common leak source; rotation + runbook makes recovery routine.

rule.statement: Verification MUST run against the raw request body bytes; JSON.parse or framework body-parsing before verify is rejected.
rule.rationale: Re-serialised JSON changes whitespace + key order, breaking signature; verify on raw bytes is the only correct path.

rule.statement: Edge layer (CDN/API gateway) MUST cap webhook endpoint at 200 req/min per source IP; bursts above MUST 429 with retry-after.

rationale.source: OWASP - rate limiting; Stripe IP allowlist guidance

rule.rationale: Brute-force / forge-attempts saturate the verify path; edge cap protects compute + idempotency store.

rule.statement: Every failed verification MUST emit a structured audit log (timestamp, source IP, error reason) with >=90 days retention.
rule.rationale: Forged-event attempts are early warning; without an audit they go unnoticed until a real breach.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if every entry in `## Applies If` holds and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec - decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: Misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; explicit skip rule is the universal default branch.
